steps.title: Identify what the goal requires
steps.content: To emphasize a difference between the meters, we need to contrast what structurally defines each meter—not languages or vague definitions, but the actual building blocks (sounds vs. syllable length).

steps.title: Select the choice showing the meters' structural difference
steps.content:
The lines of the poem Meghadūta use a pattern of long and short syllables, whereas Widsith's lines use a pattern of repeated sounds. identifies what creates each meter—Meghadūta's long and short syllables versus Widsith's repeated sounds—the precise contrast that reveals how the meters differ.

The others either abandon the meters for languages, mischaracterize what quantitative meter is, or swap which poem uses which pattern.

That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
